practical_description: The gold standard for soft body armor. Catches and deforms bullets with fiber tension. Poor against blades or sharp fragments. Delaminates after repeat strain.
visual_description: Tightly woven golden-yellow fabric, stiff under pressure. Slightly waxy touch with visible yarn patterns.

practical_description: Ultra-light alternative to Kevlar. Absorbs blunt energy well but suffers under heat and repeated compression. Used in covert and marine armor.
visual_description: White synthetic fabric with glossy crisscross threads. Smooth, semi-slick surface, almost like hard sailcloth.

practical_description: Composite armor for edged/blunt crossover threats. Solid against fast, small projectiles. STF hardens briefly, then relaxes. Performance drops fast under repeated abuse.
visual_description: Kevlar sheet with embedded glossy finish. Slight stick to the hand. Waxes over time, grays with wear.

practical_description: Impact-tolerant soft armor with rubbery bounce. Excellent for riot use and limb panels. Warps and tears rather than cracking.
visual_description: Dull yellow core encased in semi-gloss black skin. Compresses and rebounds when squeezed. Slight chemical odor.

practical_description: Carbon nanotube-based elastic webbing. Deflects glancing hits and surface-level trauma. Tension disperses, but edge cuts penetrate easily.
visual_description: Midnight black matte fabric. Flexes like latex, but with powder-dry finish. Sparkles faintly at stretch points.

practical_description: Premium-tier soft armor. High density without weight penalty. Slows even rifle threats after impact reshaping. Expensive and fragile to heat.
visual_description: Dark grey with graphite speckle. Dense but flowing. Light pools in diagonal threads like carbon velvet.

practical_description: Old but serviceable. Catches basic frag and debris. Best used in layering or against low-velocity threats. Cuts and frays easily.
visual_description: Thick black textile with coarse weave. Frays at the edges. Looks surplus, smells of storage.

practical_description: Non-lethal armor pad. Blunts baton strikes, absorbs shock. Poor against ballistics. Found in crowd-control gear and shields.
visual_description: Thick rubber slab with embedded mesh. Feels like a tire tread. Matte and scuffed with pressure wrinkles.
